figure.title: Ack and Release Latency Loop
figure.purpose: Illustrates the round-trip delay components involved in transmitting a payload and receiving its acknowledgment to release the Tx Buffer.

technical_summary: The left node transmits a payload from Tx DL through Tx PL, traversing amplifiers to the right node (Rx PL -> Rx DL).
The right node processes the payload and generates an 'Ack Request'.
The right node transmits the Ack from Tx DL through Tx PL back over the physical medium to the left node (Rx PL -> Rx DL).
The left node's Rx DL then issues a 'Release TxBuffer' signal to its Tx DL.
The diagram highlights two critical latency paths: 'Release Latency' (total round trip) and 'Ack Latency' (time taken at the remote node to generate the Ack).

components.name: Tx DL / Rx DL
components.description: Data Link layer transmit and receive blocks.

components.name: Tx PL / Rx PL
components.description: Physical layer transmit and receive blocks.

constraints: Release Latency > Round Trip Flight Time + Processing Time

critical_facts: The total time to release a buffer in the Tx Replay queue depends on the sum of flight times in both directions plus the remote node's processing latency (Ack Latency).
